subject: Re: Error with Reconstructing Sub-Tomograms at Binned Pixel Size
body:
Hi Thomas,
You are likely to have more success with using relion 3.1 than 4.0. Is
there any particular reason for choosing 4 over 3.1? The utility of
relion in this pipeline is to only find the optimal poses of the
particles while M does most of the heavy lifting in terms of
corrections. As such using even older versions of relion (such 3.0)
will be sufficient.

TL;DR - Dont actively make the problem harder for yourself when
simpler solutions exist ;)
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK


On Wed, Sep 14, 2022 at 9:52 PM Tommy Newton <tn3wton12@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Hello,
>
> I have been working on the TeamTomo tutorial and have run into trouble in the "Particle Pose Optimization" section.
>
> I was successfully able to run the 3D auto-refine in Relion. However, I am using Relion 4.0, not Relion 3.1 as the tutorial was written for. I ran the command below to convert the file:
>
> relion_star_downgrade -s run_it023_data.star
>
> When moving over to Warp to reconstruct the sub-tomograms at 1.6Å/px, I selected this file and entered the parameters as displayed on the tutorial. The system that I am using runs on two GPUs, so the reconstruction process opened up terminals for both GPUs to track the process.
>
> The first problem is that the terminal reads out that there are over 148,000 particles needing to be exported, despite the .star file only showing that there are around 12,500 particles to be used. The second problem is that Warp crashes after about 5 minutes of processing. The error begins with "System.Exception: Couldn't export the particles!" One of the terminals showed that the GPU was out of memory, but that was not true after looking into the system. The error that showed in that terminal was along the lines of what Fadwa experienced in a previous forum post from August 2020, starting with "System.Exception: cudaErrorMemoryAllocation," but I had already defined that the memory should not be a problem.
>
> I first tried to see if the .star files had all the necessary components (i.e, _rlnCoordinateX, Y, etc), comparing it to that of the .star file that was used for the original sub-tomogram reconstruction before 3D auto refine. All necessary factors were contained in the file upon that inspection.
>
> I then tried to downgrade the file with the relion_convert_star command, but that produced an empty file.
>
> Finally, I tested the original 'result_10Apx_nodup_neighbourcleaning_data.star' file from the first round of sub-tomogram reconstruction with the parameters at the step for 1.6Å/px, and that failed as well.
>
> Is there a different means by which I should be converting the Relion 4.0 .star file to a Relion 3.0 .star file? Or is there a problem with the parameters in Warp (I am using 1.0.9 by the way)?
>
> Thank you, and please let me know if there is any other information that you need me to provide.
>
> Thomas
>
> --
